latest_messages.headline: Pre-market plan: holding all 5, no buys — jobs report today, holiday Friday
latest_messages.body: Research-only pre-market. All five theses intact, all safety-sells resting, book fully green (unrealized ~+$4,952). No trades planned: at our 5-stock cap, nothing on the bench clears the buy bar (Goldman still the best fresh idea at 58/100, one signal short), and no holding is near a stop. The June jobs report prints 8:30 ET into thin, pre-holiday trading (closed Friday for July 4th) — I won't add risk into that number. A helpful shift: money is rotating into industrials/energy (good for GE, Deere, Eaton). Today doubles as our weekly review — I'll weigh whether ~34% cash is too cautious.

latest_messages.headline: Close: equity $103,850 · day -0.30% · SPY -0.13% · alpha -0.16%
latest_messages.body: Quiet, do-nothing day; the market dipped and we dipped a touch more (cash cushion + steadier stocks mute both directions). No trades — at our 5-stock cap, nothing near a stop. Book fully green with +$4,858 unrealized: Eli Lilly led (+10.5%) as its Medicare weight-loss program starts today; GE held +13.7%. Still trailing the market this week (+0.71% vs SPY +2.24%) on cash drag. Jobs report Thursday, then closed Friday for July 4th; I won't add risk into that number.

latest_messages.headline: Midday — steady, no changes; the morning dip healed and all 5 holdings are now green
latest_messages.body: Re-checked all five holdings and every safety-sell; nothing needed action. The morning dip reversed — Eaton went from -2.6% to +4.4%, and JPMorgan flipped from red to +1.4%, so the whole book is green (unrealized ~+$4,939). Still -$228 on the day (-0.22%) as the broader market outpaces our ~34% cash cushion. JPM now ~3.5% from its stop-upgrade trigger. At the 5-stock cap; won't add risk into Thursday's jobs report. Close routine owns the official scorecard.

latest_messages.headline: Open: no trades — holding all 5; down a touch (-$227, -0.22%) on normal morning noise
latest_messages.body: Made no trades at the bell. We're at our 5-stock cap and nothing new clears the 2-signal bar, so no buys; all five theses are intact and none near a safety-sell, so no sells. Eaton (-2.6%) and Deere (-1%) dipped ahead of Thursday's jobs report; Lilly and GE are green. Watching Eaton's trailing stop, now ~5% below its price. ~34% cash. Re-check at midday.

latest_messages.headline: Morning plan: nothing to buy or sell; all 5 stocks fine, two open questions answered
latest_messages.body: No trades (research-only pre-market). All five theses intact, all safety-sells resting. Two flags cleared in our favor: Eli Lilly's Medicare $50/mo weight-loss program starts this year (2026, confirmed), and JPMorgan's $50B buyback is officially live (SEC filing). No new idea clears our 2-signal bar — Goldman is the best fresh name (dividend hike) but goes on the bench. Full at 5 stocks; won't add risk into Thursday's jobs report; closed Friday for the holiday.

latest_messages.headline: Close: equity $104,125 · day +0.07% · SPY +0.78% · alpha -0.72%
latest_messages.body: Quiet, slightly-green day; the market beat us. No trades (at the 5-stock cap, nothing near a stop). Eaton +4.4% (+$429) offset Eli Lilly -2.5% (-$424). Trailing the index this week (we're +0.97% vs SPY +2.37%) on cash drag + lower beta. Closed Friday for the holiday; jobs report Thursday.

latest_messages.headline: Midday — steady, no changes; all 5 green and Deere kept climbing
latest_messages.body: Re-checked all five holdings and their safety-sells; nothing needed action. Deere's trailing stop auto-ratcheted higher as it rose. JPM is flat (-0.1%), nothing near a stop, at our 5-stock cap so nothing to buy. Up +0.14% on the day; trailing a stronger market purely on cash drag. Close routine owns today's official scorecard.

latest_messages.headline: Open: no trades — holding all 5, nothing new clears the buy bar
latest_messages.body: Made no trades at the bell. At our 5-stock max, and nothing new scores high enough to replace a holding. No sells either — all five theses intact, none near a safety-sell. JPM (day-2 starter) is the lone red at -0.7%, normal noise. ~34% cash. Re-check at midday.
